frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Yaar, honestly, I get that women should be able to go anywhere at any time, but let's be realistic. Pune after 10-11 PM, especially some areas, is just not safe. It's not about what *should* be, it's about what *is*. So, for your own safety, it's always better to avoid going out alone or being in isolated spots. I tell my sister the same thing – apna dhyan toh khud hi rakhna padta hai. Zamaana hi kharab hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment, from a young male software engineer, is misogynistic because it frames women's safety as their sole responsibility, advising them to restrict their movement rather than addressing male violence. It uses the 'just being practical' defense and the 'zamaana kharab hai' justification, masking victim-blaming as protective advice.
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 27. Single. A software engineer in Pune. You are posting anonymously on Reddit after your male colleague told you that his girlfriend of three years cheated on him with their mutual friend. You are writing a comment about what this says about women in general and whether men can ever fully trust women in relationships.
